name: ProposalToTasks
description: Reads an approved proposal and produces exactly one canonical dependency-aware task tracker used by task execution. Runs until the tracker is fully written and verified - do not set a manual iteration limit when invoking this loop. Pass the relative path to the proposal as the prompt.
startPrompt:
You are driving a proposal-to-tasks pipeline to completion. Work through the phases below in order. Do not skip phases. Do not stop early.

Proposal path (relative to WorkSource): {userPrompt}

This loop is the second loop in the default workflow. Its output is exactly one canonical task tracker used by task execution. Do not create an implementation plan, an execution plan, or any companion planning artifact.

----------------------------------------
PHASE 1 - READ
----------------------------------------
Use read_context to read the full proposal at the path above.
Then use read_context to read Templates/TaskTrackerTemplate.md.
Do not write anything in this phase - reading only.
When done, output a one-line summary: "READ COMPLETE - <proposal title>, <N> phases/sections found."

----------------------------------------
PHASE 2 - DECOMPOSE
----------------------------------------
Break the proposal into tasks. Rules:
- One task per independently executable unit of work.
- Do NOT invent scope not stated in the proposal.
- Every task must have: short imperative title, 1-3 sentence description referencing specific files/components named in the proposal, priority (High/Medium/Low), effort estimate (30m/1h/2h/4h/1d/2d/3d+), status set to `Not Started`, owner placeholder, explicit dependencies, and a specific verifiable done-condition.
- Every task must declare `Dependencies`. Use `-` when there are none.
- Use only these task status labels anywhere in the tracker: `Not Started`, `In Progress`, `Blocked`, `Complete`.
- Order tasks by dependency within each phase: prerequisites first.
- Only declare a dependency when a task truly cannot begin until another task is complete.
- Group by phase when the proposal defines phases; use a flat list otherwise.
- Prepare progress counts that match the initial task states.
Output the full decomposed task list in plain text before writing anything.

----------------------------------------
PHASE 3 - WRITE
----------------------------------------
Use write_document to create the task tracker file.
  Output path: same directory as the source proposal, filename = <ProposalBaseName>Tasks.md
  Example: Projects/Proposals/AsyncExecutionProposal.md -> Projects/Proposals/AsyncExecutionProposalTasks.md
  Write exactly one tracker file and no other document.
  Content: follow TaskTrackerTemplate.md exactly -
    - Header block (source proposal link, status=Active, created date, last updated date, owner)
    - One-sentence summary
    - Task table(s) with Status, Owner, Dependencies, and Done-Condition columns
    - Task State Rules section
    - Dependency Rules section
    - Done-Condition Rules section
    - Mermaid dependency map only when tasks have non-trivial dependencies
    - Blockers & Notes section only when blockers or notable execution notes exist
    - Progress Summary table with one row per phase and a required `Total` row
After the write_document action, output: "WRITE COMPLETE - <output path>"

----------------------------------------
PHASE 4 - VERIFY
----------------------------------------
Use read_context to read back the file you just wrote.
Check every item in this list:
  [ ] Exactly one `*Tasks.md` tracker was written and no other planning artifact was created
  [ ] Header block present with all required fields
  [ ] Source proposal link is correct
  [ ] Every task has: title, description, priority, effort, status (`Not Started`), owner placeholder, dependencies, and done-condition
  [ ] Every task declares `Dependencies` using task numbers or `-`
  [ ] Only canonical status labels are used
  [ ] Tasks are ordered dependency-first within each phase
  [ ] Progress Summary includes every phase row plus a `Total` row
  [ ] Progress Summary totals match the actual task counts
  [ ] No tasks invented beyond what the proposal stated
  [ ] File follows TaskTrackerTemplate.md structure exactly

If any check fails:
  - Use write_document to overwrite the file with all issues corrected.
  - List each fix you made.
  - Re-run the checklist against the corrected version before proceeding.

If all checks pass, output:
  "VERIFY COMPLETE - all checks passed. Tracker written to <output path>."
  Then output the keyword: TASKS_COMPLETE

Do not output TASKS_COMPLETE until VERIFY COMPLETE has been confirmed.
